content.description: A unique fact is: Time passes measurably slower near a black hole due to gravitational time dilation — a clock at the event horizon would appear to stop entirely when observed from a distance.

metadata.reasoning: Derived from Einstein's General Relativity, experimentally confirmed via GPS satellite clock corrections and gravitational wave observations.

trajectory.detail: Considered multiple black hole properties — singularity, event horizon, Hawking radiation, time dilation
Selected time dilation as it is counterintuitive and directly observable in principle
Confirmed claim aligns with GR predictions and experimental evidence
